reasoning: 18 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200 is extremely rare, nearly unprecedented in the modern tracking era. Even without knowing the artist, the base rate for an album achieving 18 consecutive weeks at #1 is extremely close to 0%. Major releases constantly debut at #1, typically breaking any multi-week streaks. Morgan Wallen's 'One Thing At A Time' had a huge run but not 18 consecutive weeks, and Taylor Swift albums usually have breaks. Therefore, the probability is practically zero.
